title: APT Package Manifest
description: Defines Debian packages to build from source or download from system repositories.

entries.description: List of APT packages to manage.

name.description: Logical package name used in logs and the TUI.

version.description: Package version. Used in build paths and S3 keys to allow multiple versions.

source_name.description: Upstream package or directory name. Defaults to name when absent.

url.description: Git URL to clone for a source build. When omitted, the package is fetched via apt-get download.

build_cmd.description: Shell command to run from the package root (cloned source directory). The working directory is the repo root, so 'build-deb.sh' is sufficient — no './' prefix needed.

deb_glob.description: Glob pattern (relative to source dir) to locate the built .deb file.

frozen.description: When true, the entry cannot be built, edited, or deleted.
